login.title: Fluux Messenger
login.subtitle: Connect to your XMPP server
login.serverHint: Leave empty to use domain from JID
login.serverHintDesktop: Leave empty to auto-detect. Supports domain, tls://host:port, tcp://host:port

sidebar.resizeHint: Drag to resize, double-click to reset

conversations.deleteConfirmMessage: The message history will be permanently deleted.

contacts.subscriptionNote: A subscription request will be sent. The contact will appear in your roster once they accept.
contacts.blockConfirm: Block {{name}}? They won't be able to contact you.
contacts.keyboardHint: Tab/↑↓ to navigate, Enter to select

encryption.removeVerificationConfirm: Remove verification for {{name}}'s key? You'll need to re-verify via a second channel.
encryption.notAvailableDescription: This contact has not published an OpenPGP key. Messages will be sent in plaintext.
encryption.disabledByYouDescription: You disabled encryption for this contact. Messages will be sent in plaintext.
encryption.unavailableNowDescription: Reconnect or enable encryption in settings.
encryption.rejectedDescription: This contact has published an OpenPGP key, but it failed validation. Messages will be sent in plaintext.

rooms.nicknamePlaceholder: Your display name in the room
rooms.autoJoinDescription: Automatically join this room on connect
rooms.joinRoomHint: Enter the full room address to join a public chat room.
rooms.cachedHistoryWarning: Cached history up to {{date}}. Join to get latest messages.
rooms.notJoinedNoHistory: You are not in this room
rooms.quickChatDescription: Create a temporary room that disappears when everyone leaves.
rooms.avatarChangeFailed: Failed to change room avatar. Only room owners can change the avatar.
rooms.avatarClearFailed: Failed to remove room avatar. Only room owners can remove the avatar.
rooms.banConfirm: Ban {{jid}} from the room? They will not be able to rejoin.
rooms.invalidMucService: Please enter a valid MUC service address
rooms.browseRoomsHint: {{count}} public rooms available on this server
rooms.subject: Subject
rooms.fetchConfigError: Failed to load room configuration. You may not have owner permissions.
rooms.destroyRoomConfirm: This will permanently destroy the room and remove all members. This action cannot be undone.
rooms.templatePrivateHelp: The room is hidden from the directory. Only people you invite can join. Best for team or group conversations.
rooms.templateOpenHelp: The room is listed in the public directory. Anyone can find it and join without an invitation. Best for community channels.
rooms.templateUnlistedHelp: The room is hidden from the directory, but anyone who has the address can join freely. Best for sharing a link to a room.
rooms.createError: Failed to create room. The room address may already be taken.
rooms.roomAlreadyExists: A room with this address already exists. Please choose a different address.
rooms.destroyHatConfirm: Delete hat "{{title}}"? It will be removed from all assigned users.
rooms.noHats: No hats defined for this room

chat.deleteMessageConfirm: This will delete this message. This action cannot be undone.
chat.moderateMessageConfirm: This will remove this message for all participants. This action cannot be undone.
chat.moderateReasonPlaceholder: Why is this message being removed?
chat.moderateAndBan: Also ban this user from the room

encryption.blockedTooltip: Peer's key has changed — resolve to resume encryption
encryption.plaintextForcedTooltip: You've disabled encryption for this conversation — messages are sent in plaintext
encryption.encryptedDisabled: Encryption disabled — enable it in the settings
encryption.couldNotDecryptTooltip: Message encrypted to a key not available on this device
encryption.rejectedTooltip: Peer's OpenPGP certificate is invalid — click for details

rejectionCode.fingerprint_mismatch: Fingerprint does not match advertised value
rejectionCode.uid_mismatch: Certificate identity does not match peer's address

encryption.sendBlockedEncryptionRequired: Message not sent — encryption is required for this conversation but isn't available right now.
encryption.sendBlockedKeyChanged: Message not sent — this contact's encryption key changed. Review the key change before sending.
encryption.sendBlockedKeyLocked: Message not sent — unlock your encryption key to send this message.
encryption.sendBlockedKeyConflict: Message not sent — resolve your encryption key conflict before sending.

verifyPeer.chipAriaLabel: Encrypted to {{name}} — click to verify fingerprint
verifyPeer.dialogBody: Read your code aloud to {{name}} and type the code they read to you. The codes only match if no one is intercepting your messages.
verifyPeer.dialogWarning: Use a trusted second channel — in person, a video call you recognise the person on, an already-verified end-to-end-encrypted chat. The server can't be trusted to relay this comparison for you.
verifyPeer.theirCodeLabel: Enter the 4 digits {{name}} reads to you
verifyPeer.theirCodeMismatch: Code doesn't match — ask {{name}} to read it again
verifyPeer.codeUnavailable: Verification code unavailable — close the dialog and try again in a moment.
verifyPeer.ownFingerprintUnavailable: Your fingerprint isn't available yet — close the dialog and try again in a moment.
verifyPeer.alreadyVerifiedBanner: This key has already been verified. You can re-verify if you have any doubts.

keyChangeBanner.title: {{name}}'s encryption key has changed
keyChangeBanner.body: This contact appears to have rotated their encryption key. You can verify the new fingerprint to restore full trust, or accept it without verifying to continue with basic trust.
keyChangeBanner.encryptionBlocked: Encryption is paused for this conversation until you resolve the change.
keyChangeBanner.acceptedWithoutVerifying: New key accepted — peer is unverified
keyChangeBanner.acceptFailed: Couldn't accept the new key — try again

settings.title: Settings

encryption.openpgpDescription: Publish a public key to your server and encrypt 1:1 messages to contacts who also have one.
encryption.statusReady: Your key is ready and published.
encryption.statusGenerationFailed: Key generation failed. Check the developer console for details.
encryption.probeFailed: Couldn't reach the server to check for an existing backup. Try again — nothing was changed.
encryption.limitationBackend: Crypto backend not independently audited — not recommended for very high-sensitivity use cases.
encryption.deleteKeyDescription: Permanently removes your private key from this device. Messages previously encrypted to this key become unreadable. Encryption is turned off until you generate a new key.
encryption.deleteKeyConfirmMessage: This destroys the private key for fingerprint {{fingerprint}}. You won't be able to decrypt past messages. This action cannot be undone.
encryption.deleteKeyConsequences: Your public key will be retracted from the server so contacts stop encrypting to it, and the private key will be wiped from this device.
encryption.deleteKeyAlsoBackupHint: Keep it unchecked if you may want to restore this identity on another device later.
encryption.backupDescription: Publish an encrypted copy of your key to your XMPP server. Protected by a passphrase only you know, and readable only by your account (access model: whitelist). Lets you read encrypted history on a new device after signing in and entering the passphrase.
encryption.backupDialogBody: A passphrase has been generated to protect your key. Write it down or store it in a password manager — you'll need it when restoring on another device.
encryption.backupDialogWarning: If you lose this passphrase, the backup is unrecoverable and your encrypted history becomes unreadable on a new device. Save it now.
encryption.backupAcknowledgment: I've saved this passphrase somewhere safe.
encryption.savePassphraseManagerFallback: Passphrase copied — paste it into your password manager
encryption.backupStatusNone: Not backed up on this server yet.
encryption.backupStatusInSync: This key is backed up on the server.
encryption.backupStatusMismatch: A backup exists on the server, but it doesn't match this device's key.
encryption.restoreDialogTitle: Restore your OpenPGP key from backup
encryption.restoreDialogBody: Enter the passphrase you saved when you created this backup. The restored key will replace the one currently stored on this device.
encryption.restoreDialogWarning: Any encrypted messages sent since the backup was created — addressed only to the key on this device — will remain unreadable after the restore. Encrypt-to-all-devices is not supported by OpenPGP for XMPP.

keyPicker.title: Multiple keys found
keyPicker.body: The backup contains multiple keys. Select the one you want to use.

encryption.backupConflictMessage: A backup is already stored on this server, but it wasn't created from this device. Replacing it makes the old backup unrecoverable — anyone holding only its passphrase will no longer be able to restore from it. Continue if you're sure you don't need that copy any more.
encryption.rotateDescription: Generates a fresh encryption subkey. Your identity (fingerprint) stays the same — contacts won't need to re-verify you. Past messages remain readable on this device. New messages will use the new subkey.
encryption.rotateConfirmMessage: A new encryption subkey will be generated and published. Your identity stays the same. Past encrypted messages remain readable on this device.
encryption.rotateConfirmMessageWithBackup: A new encryption subkey will be generated and published. Your server backup will be re-encrypted with a new passphrase — the old passphrase will no longer work. Past encrypted messages remain readable on this device.
encryption.rotateFailed: Couldn't rotate the key. Try again.

trustStateCompromised.title: Trust state integrity check failed
trustStateCompromised.description: Local trust data may have been tampered with. Silent key re-pinning is blocked to prevent key substitution.

ownKeyConflict.primaryMismatchBody: The key the server is advertising for your account doesn't match the one on this device. This could mean the server was tampered with, or another device generated a new key.
ownKeyConflict.subkeyMismatchTitle: Your key was updated on another device
ownKeyConflict.subkeyMismatchBody: The server has a newer version of your key — another device rotated the encryption subkey. Import the backup to use the latest version here, or keep this device's key if you prefer.
ownKeyConflict.encryptionBlocked: Encryption is paused until you resolve this conflict.
ownKeyConflict.overwriteSuccess: Your key has been re-published to the server.
ownKeyConflict.overwriteFailed: Couldn't re-publish your key. Try again.

identityChoice.title: An OpenPGP identity already exists for this account
identityChoice.body: This browser has no local key, but the server already advertises one for your account. Generating a new key here would silently fork your identity — peers who encrypted to the existing key would no longer be able to deliver messages. Pick how you want to resolve this:
identityChoice.restoreFromServerBody: Use the backup passphrase you set when enabling encryption on another device. The matching private key is fetched from the server and unlocked locally.
identityChoice.restoreFromServerUnavailable: No secret-key backup is published on the server. Use another option to recover this account's identity.
identityChoice.importFromFileBody: Choose a key export file you saved from another device. You will be asked for the passphrase that protects it.
identityChoice.replaceBody: Retract the existing public key and generate a brand-new one. Use only if you cannot recover the matching private key.
identityChoice.replaceWarning: This is destructive: every peer who has pinned your current fingerprint will see a key-change alert and must re-verify, and any past message encrypted to the retired key becomes unreadable on this device.

encryption.exportFileDialogTitle: Export your OpenPGP key to file
encryption.exportFileDialogBody: A passphrase will protect your exported key file. Write it down or store it in a password manager — you will need it when importing the key on another device.
encryption.importFileDialogBody: Enter the passphrase that protects this key file. The file can be a Fluux backup (created with "Export to file") or a GnuPG private key exported with `gpg --export-secret-keys --armor`. The imported key will replace the one currently stored on this device.
encryption.importFileFailed: Could not read the selected file
encryption.unsupportedKeyAlgorithm: Imported key uses an algorithm that is no longer supported (e.g. DSA or ElGamal). Use a modern OpenPGP key (Curve25519, Ed25519, or RSA at least 2048 bits).
encryption.externalExportDialogTitle: Export private key for an external OpenPGP tool
encryption.externalExportDialogBody: Produces a standard OpenPGP private key file (PRIVATE KEY BLOCK) that you can import into gpg, OpenKeychain, Kleopatra, and other tools.
encryption.externalExportProtectedDescription: Recommended. Pick a passphrase; the exported file is encrypted and external tools will ask for it on import.
encryption.externalExportUnprotectedDescription: The exported file contains your private key in clear text. Use only if your target tool refuses passphrase-protected keys.
encryption.externalExportUnprotectedWarning: Without a passphrase, anyone with access to the exported file can impersonate you and decrypt your past and future messages. Delete the file as soon as you have imported it.
encryption.externalExportUnprotectedAcknowledgment: I understand the risks and want to export my private key unencrypted.
encryption.unlockDialogBody: Enter your passphrase to decrypt your encryption key for this session.
encryption.unlockDialogSetupBody: Choose a passphrase to protect your encryption key. You will need it each time you sign in.
encryption.unlockPassphraseTooShort: Passphrase must be at least 8 characters
encryption.lockedBannerBody: Your encryption key is locked. Enter your passphrase to enable encryption for this session.
encryption.rotateNotSupportedWeb: Key rotation is not available on the web — use the desktop app.

blocked.title: Blocked Users
blocked.description: Blocked contacts cannot send you messages or see your presence.
blocked.noResults: No blocked users match your search
blocked.invalidJid: Please enter a valid JID (e.g., user@example.com)

settings.snippetsDescription: Snippets are CSS files that customize the app appearance
settings.fontSizeDescription: Adjust the size of text across the app
settings.languageStreamNote: Server language preference applies on next login
settings.notificationDescription: Desktop notifications alert you to new messages when the app is in the background.

storage.title: Storage
storage.mediaCacheDescription: Downloaded images and media are cached locally to avoid re-downloading them.
storage.searchIndexDescription: The search index allows full-text search across your message history. Rebuild it if search results seem incomplete.

update.title: Update Available
update.errorNetwork: Unable to reach update server. Check your internet connection.

menu.logoutConfirm: Are you sure you want to disconnect?

about.title: About
about.description: A powerful, productive messaging client that's pleasant to use.

profile.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 6 characters
profile.passwordChangeNotSupported: Password change is not supported by this server
profile.offlineNotice: Profile editing is unavailable while offline

console.title: XMPP Console
console.enterStanza: Enter XML stanza to send... ({{modifier}}+Enter to send)

messages.title: Private Conversations
messages.description: Select a conversation from the sidebar or create one from your contact list.

rooms.title: Chat Rooms
rooms.description: Join a room from your bookmarks or create a new group chat to start chatting.

directory.title: Contact Information
directory.description: Click on a contact to view their profile, connected devices, or start a conversation.
directory.hint: Double-click to start a conversation directly.

archive.title: Archived Conversations
archive.description: Archived conversations will appear here. You can restore them to your message list anytime.

events.title: Pending Events
events.description: Subscription requests, room invitations, and messages from unknown contacts will appear here.

admin.title: Server Administration
admin.description: Select a command from the sidebar to manage your server.

search.title: Search Results
search.description: Select a result from the sidebar to view the conversation.

avatar.invalidFileType: Please select a PNG, JPEG, GIF, or WebP image
avatar.fileTooLarge: File is too large. Maximum size is 5MB.
avatar.cameraNotFound: No camera found on this device
avatar.cameraAccessDenied: Camera access denied. Please enable camera permissions.
avatar.webcamNotAvailable: Webcam is not available in this context
avatar.dragOrClick: Drag image here or click to upload
avatar.takePhotoDescription: Take a photo with your camera

upload.notSupported: File upload is not supported by this server

admin.title: Server Administration

noAccess.title: Admin Access Required
noAccess.description: You don't have administrator privileges on this server. Contact your server administrator for access.

admin.selectCommand: Select a command from the sidebar

userView.title: User
userView.confirmDeleteMessage: Are you sure you want to delete {{jid}}? This action cannot be undone.
userView.confirmEndSessionsMessage: Are you sure you want to end all sessions for {{jid}}? The user will be disconnected immediately.

roomView.confirmDestroyMessage: Are you sure you want to destroy "{{room}}"? This will kick all occupants and delete the room permanently.

dataFormHints.muc#roomconfig_roomname: The display name shown in room listings and the header.
dataFormHints.muc#roomconfig_roomdesc: A short description of the room's purpose.
dataFormHints.muc#roomconfig_persistentroom: Persistent rooms remain even when all participants leave. Non-persistent rooms are destroyed when empty.
dataFormHints.muc#roomconfig_publicroom: Public rooms appear in the directory. Private rooms can only be found by those who know the address.
dataFormHints.muc#roomconfig_membersonly: When enabled, only users on the member list can join. Others must be invited.
dataFormHints.muc#roomconfig_allowinvites: Allow participants to invite others to the room.
dataFormHints.muc#roomconfig_changesubject: Allow participants to change the room subject.
dataFormHints.muc#roomconfig_moderatedroom: In a moderated room, only moderators and above can send messages unless granted voice.
dataFormHints.muc#roomconfig_whois: Controls who can see participants' real JID (address). Moderators only or anyone.
dataFormHints.muc#roomconfig_passwordprotectedroom: Require a password to join the room.
dataFormHints.muc#roomconfig_roomsecret: The password required to join the room.
dataFormHints.muc#roomconfig_maxusers: Maximum number of participants allowed in the room.
dataFormHints.muc#roomconfig_enablelogging: Enable public logging of conversations. Transcripts may be made visible outside the room (e.g. on a website). This is different from message archive (MAM) used by clients to fetch history.
dataFormHints.muc#roomconfig_lang: The primary language used in this room.
dataFormHints.muc#roomconfig_allowpm: Control who can send private messages to other participants.
dataFormHints.muc#roomconfig_presencebroadcast: Which roles receive presence notifications when participants join or leave.
dataFormHints.muc#roomconfig_pubsub: Associate a PubSub node with this room for extended features.
dataFormHints.public_list: When enabled, anyone can see who is in the room. When disabled, only participants can see the occupant list.
dataFormHints.members_by_default: New users joining the room automatically get participant role, allowing them to speak in moderated rooms.
dataFormHints.allow_private_messages_from_visitors: Controls whether visitors (users without voice) can send private messages.
dataFormHints.allow_query_users: Allow users to send information queries (service discovery) to other participants.
dataFormHints.allow_visitor_status: Allow visitors to include a status message with their presence.
dataFormHints.allow_visitor_nickchange: Allow visitors to change their nickname in the room.
dataFormHints.allow_voice_requests: Allow visitors in a moderated room to request permission to speak.
dataFormHints.allow_subscription: Allow users to subscribe to room messages without being present. Subscribers receive messages without joining the room, useful for mobile clients or notifications.
dataFormHints.voice_request_min_interval: Minimum wait time (in seconds) between voice requests from the same visitor.
dataFormHints.enable_hats: Enable badge display next to participant names, showing special roles or affiliations.
dataFormHints.mam: Enable message archive (MAM) so clients can fetch past messages when joining or reconnecting. This is the history participants see in their chat app.

tabCoordination.alreadyOpen: Fluux is already open in another tab
tabCoordination.alreadyOpenDescription: Only one tab can be connected at a time to prevent message conflicts.
tabCoordination.takenOverDescription: This tab was disconnected because Fluux was opened in another tab.

poll.title: Title
poll.bannerMultiple: 📊 {{count}} unanswered polls — tap to see latest
